verificationStatus: not-verified-against-gazette — this risk management methodology is a constructed representation based on Act 854 requirements and international standards; it has not been verified against any officially published NACSA instrument
title: NCII Cyber Security Risk Assessment Methodology
description: Risk assessment methodology for NCII operators under the Cyber Security Act 2024 (Act 854), aligned with ISO 31000:2018, ISO 27005:2022, and the NACSA Directive on Risk Assessment Methodology. Designed for entities designated under s17 fulfilling obligations under s21.
applicableTo: All NCII entities designated under s17 of Act 854
approvalAuthority: CISO or NCII Compliance Officer (with board endorsement for Critical/High residual risk acceptance)

reviewCycle.frequency: Annual mandatory review, plus triggered review after significant changes
triggers: Major cyber security incident affecting NCII systems
Significant change to NCII infrastructure or architecture
New NACSA directive, regulation, or code of practice
Material change in threat landscape (e.g., new APT campaign targeting Malaysian NCII)
Change of NCII designation scope by the Chief Executive
Introduction of new technology or OT/IoT systems into NCII scope

likelihoodScale.description: Five-level likelihood scale for assessing the probability of a threat event exploiting a vulnerability in NCII systems

levels.label: Rare
levels.description: The event is highly unlikely to occur within the assessment period. No known threat actors targeting this vector in Malaysia or the region.
levels.criteria: No historical precedent; theoretical threat only; no active campaigns observed

levels.label: Unlikely
levels.description: The event could occur but is not expected. Limited threat actor interest or capability observed.
levels.criteria: Isolated historical incidents globally; no specific targeting of Malaysian NCII; basic controls would prevent exploitation

levels.label: Possible
levels.description: The event may occur during the assessment period. Threat actors with relevant capability exist and the sector is a plausible target.
levels.criteria: Comparable incidents in the region; threat intelligence indicates sector targeting; controls may not fully address the vector

levels.label: Likely
levels.description: The event is expected to occur during the assessment period. Active threat campaigns targeting this sector or technology stack observed.
levels.criteria: Recent incidents in Malaysia or ASEAN; active exploitation of the vulnerability class; existing controls insufficient

levels.label: Almost Certain
levels.description: The event is expected to occur frequently or is already occurring. Confirmed targeting of Malaysian NCII or active exploitation observed.
levels.criteria: Ongoing campaigns; confirmed compromise attempts; known vulnerability with public exploit; controls absent or ineffective

impactScale.description: Five-level impact scale specific to NCII consequences, considering national security, essential service disruption, data compromise, and regulatory consequences under Act 854

levels.label: Negligible
levels.description: Minimal impact to NCII operations. No disruption to essential services. No data compromise. No regulatory consequence.
levels.serviceDisruption: No disruption to essential services; fully contained within non-critical systems
levels.dataCompromise: No sensitive data exposed; no NCII system data affected
levels.regulatoryConsequence: No NACSA reporting obligation triggered; internal remediation sufficient

levels.label: Minor
levels.description: Limited impact to NCII operations. Brief disruption recoverable within normal operations. Minor data exposure affecting non-critical systems.
levels.nationalSecurity: No direct national security impact; localized to single system or component
levels.serviceDisruption: Essential service degraded for less than 1 hour; no public impact; redundancy covers the gap
levels.dataCompromise: Limited non-sensitive data exposure; no personal data of critical infrastructure personnel
levels.regulatoryConsequence: Incident may require internal logging but unlikely to trigger s26 mandatory notification threshold

levels.label: Moderate
levels.description: Noticeable impact to NCII operations. Service disruption affecting internal users or limited external users. Potential personal data exposure.
levels.nationalSecurity: Indirect national security concern; may affect confidence in critical infrastructure
levels.serviceDisruption: Essential service disrupted for 1-24 hours; limited public impact; manual workarounds available
levels.dataCompromise: Moderate data exposure including potentially sensitive operational data; personal data of limited scope
levels.regulatoryConsequence: Likely triggers s26 notification obligation (6-hour window); NACSA and sector lead reporting required; potential for CE inquiry

levels.label: Major
levels.description: Significant impact to NCII operations. Prolonged service disruption affecting public or multiple stakeholders. Significant data compromise including sensitive NCII operational data.
levels.nationalSecurity: Direct national security concern; cross-sector dependencies may be affected; intelligence value for adversaries
levels.serviceDisruption: Essential service disrupted for 24 hours to 7 days; public impact requiring crisis communication; cascading effects to dependent services
levels.dataCompromise: Significant data compromise including NCII architecture details, security configurations, or large-scale personal data breach
levels.regulatoryConsequence: Mandatory s26 notification; CE may exercise s28 powers (access to information); potential audit/investigation under s23; media and public attention likely

levels.label: Catastrophic
levels.description: Severe impact to NCII operations with potential for national-level consequences. Prolonged outage of essential services affecting national security or public safety. Massive data breach with strategic implications.
levels.nationalSecurity: Severe national security impact; compromise of defence, intelligence, or government NCII; potential for cascading multi-sector failure
levels.serviceDisruption: Essential service disrupted for more than 7 days; widespread public impact; threat to public safety or economic stability; manual operations infeasible
levels.dataCompromise: Catastrophic data breach exposing national security information, critical infrastructure blueprints, or mass personal data affecting millions
levels.regulatoryConsequence: Immediate s26 notification; CE may exercise all Part IV enforcement powers; potential prosecution under s50-51; ministerial involvement under s11; cross-sector coordination activated

riskCalculation.residualFormula: Residual Risk = Residual Likelihood x Residual Impact (after existing controls)
riskCalculation.note: Risk scores drive treatment priority and NACSA reporting obligations. Risks scoring 16-25 (Critical) must be reported to the CE and sector lead within 14 days per the Directive on Risk Assessment Methodology.

phases.name: Preparation
phases.description: Establish assessment scope, team, and methodology. Confirm NCII asset inventory is current. Engage CE-approved assessor if required.

phases.name: Asset Identification & Valuation
phases.description: Identify and classify all NCII assets using the NACSA Asset Criticality Matrix. Map interdependencies and data flows.

phases.name: Threat Identification
phases.description: Identify applicable threats across all mandatory categories: APTs, cybercriminals, hacktivists, insiders, supply chain, natural disasters, technical failures.

phases.name: Vulnerability Assessment
phases.description: Conduct technical vulnerability assessment of all Critical and High criticality assets. Score using CVSS 3.1+.

phases.name: Risk Analysis & Evaluation
phases.description: Calculate risk levels using the 5x5 matrix. Determine inherent and residual risk ratings for each identified risk.

phases.name: Risk Treatment Planning
phases.description: Develop treatment plans for all risks rated Medium or above. Obtain board approval for acceptance of Critical/High risks.

phases.name: Reporting & Submission
phases.description: Submit risk assessment report to CE and sector lead. Fast-track Critical/High risks within 14 days. Full report per regulatory timeline.
